structure.title: Setup & Entorno de Desarrollo
structure.tagline: Python, VS Code, venv y Git listos para producción desde el día 1

theory_blocks.heading: Por qué el setup importa más de lo que crees
Componentes del stack que vamos a instalar
requirements.txt, .gitignore y README — los 3 archivos esenciales
El flujo de trabajo profesional con Git

iDo_steps.description: Verificar instalación de Python y crear carpeta del proyecto
Crear y activar entorno virtual con venv
Instalar paquetes esenciales y guardar requirements.txt
Crear repo en GitHub y subir el código

weDo_steps.instruction: Crea un archivo hello.py que imprima tu nombre, la versión de Python y la fecha actual
Crea un README.md con título, descripción, instrucciones de instalación y uso
Sube los cambios a GitHub con un commit bien escrito

youDo_project.title: Reproducible Data Environment — Tu primer repo profesional

selfCheck_questions.note: 5th question covers .env/security — maps to env-reproducibility concept

issues_found.observed_evidence: S01 has 4 exam concepts but V3 requires 8 subtopics (4 topics × 2 subtopics each). Missing: interpreter-selection, terminal-paths, git-branch-pr, ruff-config.
issues_found.expected: 8 subtopics with 3 variants each = 24 exam variants
issues_found.root_cause: V2 structure used 4 concepts per section; V3 mandates 8 (4×2)
issues_found.impact: Insufficient assessment coverage for V3 compliance
issues_found.fix: Expand to 8 subtopics by splitting T1 (Runtime → interpreter + terminal), T3 (Git → commits + branches/PR), T4 (Quality → gitignore + ruff/env). Add 4 new concept slugs + 12 new exam variants.

issues_found.observed_evidence: S01 has 3 We Do exercises. V3 requires 24 student exercises (3 per subtopic × 8 subtopics).
issues_found.expected: 24 exercises with starter, 2 hints, solution, edge cases, tests, feedback
issues_found.root_cause: V2 structure had 3 exercises per section; V3 mandates 24
issues_found.fix: Create 21 additional exercises across the 8 subtopics. Each exercise: E1 guided, E2 independent, E3 transfer.

issues_found.observed_evidence: S01 has 1 CodePlayground demo. V3 requires 8 demos (1 per subtopic).
issues_found.expected: 8 demos, one per subtopic, each executable in declared environment
issues_found.root_cause: Current CodePlayground has 1 demo per section, not per subtopic
issues_found.impact: Students can't interact with each subtopic's concept
issues_found.fix: Add 7 more demos covering: REPL, terminal paths, pip freeze, git branch, PR creation, Ruff config, .env.example
verification: Count demos in SectionView.tsx for 'setup' = 8 or create subtopic-specific demo component

issues_found.observed_evidence: S01 has 0 topic evaluations. V3 requires 4 (one per topic, each with 2 authentic tasks).
issues_found.expected: 4 topic evaluations with rubric 0-3 on correctness, robustness, maintainability, responsible use
issues_found.root_cause: V2 had selfCheck (section-level quiz) but no per-topic evaluations
issues_found.impact: No formative assessment at topic level
issues_found.fix: Create 4 topic evaluation packages, each with 2 authentic tasks + rubric

issues_found.observed_evidence: V3 S01 T1 mentions REPL, exit codes, PATH. V3 T4 mentions Ruff, .env.example. Current content doesn't cover these.
issues_found.expected: Theory covers REPL, exit codes, PATH, Ruff, .env.example
issues_found.root_cause: Existing content was written for V2 scope which was narrower
issues_found.impact: Incomplete coverage of V3 T1 and T4 topics
issues_found.fix: Add theory blocks for REPL/exit-codes/PATH (T1) and Ruff/.env.example (T4)
verification: Theory blocks cover all V3 T1-T4 subtopics

issues_found.observed_evidence: V3 S01 project increment is 'esqueleto de CP-N1-A'. Existing You Do is 'Reproducible Data Environment'.
issues_found.expected: Project increment explicitly references CP-N1-A and creates the skeleton
issues_found.root_cause: CP-N1-A concept didn't exist when S01 was written
issues_found.impact: Students don't know their repo will become CP-N1-A
issues_found.fix: Add a note in You Do context: 'Este repo es el esqueleto de CP-N1-A: Client Intake & Data Quality Script que completarás en S04.'
